Title: TaD27-B gene controls the tiller number in hexaploid wheat

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone that inhibits axillary bud outgrowth and reduces tillering
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Enzyme involved in strigolactone biosynthesis upstream of carlactone formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tested organs, highest in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strong in axillary meristems/axillary buds; also detected in young leaves, shoot apical meristem, and spikes; highest overall in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; 35S promoter (constitutive) in Arabidopsis complementation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field growth conditions; hydroponic culture; exogenous GR24; TIS108 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses tiller outgrowth; RNAi knockdown increases tiller number and axillary bud number, whereas overexpression decreases tiller number and elevates strigolactone output
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in wheat; overexpression in wheat; Arabidopsis d27 complementation; Orobanche germination bioassay of root extracts; GR24 rescue; TIS108 reversal; qRT-PCR; in situ hybridization; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious plant height change in transgenic wheat despite strong tiller effects
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to manipulate wheat architecture and tiller number via overexpression or downregulation, potentially improving ideotype without affecting plant height
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TaD27-B overexpression increased expression of TaD27-A and TaD27-D, suggesting feedback among homeologs; functional ortholog of Arabidopsis D27
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaD27-B is the major experimentally characterized wheat D27 homeolog in this study. 2. Knockdown increased tillering and overexpression reduced tillering. 3. Root-exudate bioassay and chemical treatments demonstrated that TaD27-B promotes strigolactone biosynthesis to inhibit axillary bud outgrowth.
